description:
Problem (spec attack): the worktree path is consumer-relevant (agents need it after 'bl claim') but §7 has no return channel and §6 makes plugin stdout a forward-verbatim human channel interleaved across the whole chain — not reliably machine-parseable.

Fix: delivery stages the derived path = worktree_path(binding, id) into the task frontmatter at CLAIM.PRE (not post — §14 'post never mutates the ball'; the id already exists at claim, unlike create, so pre can compute it). Then it falls out of 'bl show --json' authoritatively. stdout stays the human hint.

Not a derive-don't-store violation: the path is derivable BY THE PLUGIN but NOT by the consumer (core/agent lack the formula), so storing it is not redundant from the consumer's view — it's the only deterministic read. That dissolves the §11 'keep it derived/stateless' objection for the consumer-facing copy.

Consistency across the seal (confirmed, no extra work): if claim.post fails to materialize the worktree, §8 post-abort reverts the seal (git reset store back one commit, claimant cleared) -> the claim is REJECTED. The staged path only survives if the seal survives, which only survives if claim.post succeeds -> the task never carries a path to a nonexistent worktree. Field and worktree existence are consistent at every committed state.

Touches §11, §15 (revision-log entry).
title: Greenfield §11: stage derived worktree path into task frontmatter at claim.pre (authoritative, --json-readable)
